message:
Fix glob character detection in conflict-checking embedded Python

The embedded Python snippet in mcp-agent-mail-guard that checks whether a
reservation pattern contains glob characters was using a bare "{" in the
character set "*?[{". In the context of the embedded Python string (which
goes through format-string-like processing), a single "{" is interpreted
as a format delimiter rather than a literal character. Doubling it to "{{"
ensures the glob detection correctly matches literal "{" characters in file
reservation patterns, preventing false negatives where brace-based glob
patterns (e.g., "{foo,bar}") were incorrectly treated as simple path
prefixes instead of glob patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -484,7 +484,7 @@ def check_conflicts(paths, reservations):
                 break
             
             # Directory prefix matching for non-glob patterns
-            has_glob = any(c in pattern for c in "*?[{")
+            has_glob = any(c in pattern for c in "*?[{{")
             if not has_glob:
                 # Normal prefix check: file is inside reserved dir
                 if f.startswith(pattern + "/"):
